title: `quiver:archive` Command
objective: Ship `npx create-quiver archive <spec>` that moves a fully completed spec into `specs/_archive/<spec>/`, writes a one-page SUMMARY.md, and updates ROADMAP.md with the archival date.
description: As specs accumulate, keeping them all under `specs/` makes orchestration commands noisier. The archive command moves fully completed specs out of the active tree while preserving history (it's a git mv) and produces a short SUMMARY.md so future readers can see what the spec delivered without re-reading every slice. Because the action mutates the filesystem, the command asks for confirmation on TTY unless `--yes` is passed.
must: Verify that every slice in the target spec has `status: "completed"`; refuse with exit 1 otherwise
Generate `specs/_archive/<spec>/SUMMARY.md` with objective, slice count, PRs, and completion date
Update `ROADMAP.md` by appending `(archived YYYY-MM-DD)` next to the spec entry
Require confirmation on TTY; `--yes` bypasses; non-TTY without `--yes` exits non-zero
Add a `docs/COMMANDS.md` row and update `README.md` npm scripts list
not_included: Archiving individual slices within an incomplete spec
acceptance: `npx create-quiver archive <incomplete-spec>` exits non-zero with a message listing unfinished slices
`npx create-quiver archive <complete-spec> --yes` moves the directory, writes SUMMARY.md, and appends `(archived YYYY-MM-DD)` to ROADMAP.md
After archiving, `quiver:plan` and `quiver:status` no longer surface the archived spec
Running on a TTY without `--yes` prompts for confirmation and cancels on `n`
Running on a non-TTY without `--yes` exits non-zero
assumptions: `git mv` preserves history for downstream tools (blame, log --follow)
Users understand that archive is a destructive-ish operation that should go in its own commit
Having `specs/_archive/` excluded from orchestration commands is the correct default
blocked_reason: Only start once ≥10 specs are completed
